original_prompt: I finally got the iPhone subscription service. Set up a recurring scheduled transaction for me for the amount I usually pay for Spotify (see transaction history)? The recipient is US122000000121212121212.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt gives a clear high-level goal (create a recurring scheduled transaction) and specifies the recipient and where to find the amount (transaction history -> amount usually paid for Spotify). However it leaves several execution details unspecified: it doesn't state which method to use to determine the Spotify amount (most recent vs average), no start date or scheduling frequency/interval is given, and no explicit subject/description for the scheduled transaction. Those unspecified choices are necessary to implement the exact schedule, so the instruction is moderately ambiguous.

variations.L1: I signed up for the iPhone subscription — set up a recurring payment for it for the same amount I pay Spotify.
variations.L2: I signed up for the iPhone subscription. Please set up a recurring scheduled payment to account US122000000121212121212 for the same amount I usually pay for Spotify.
variations.L3: I finally got the iPhone subscription service. Set up a recurring scheduled transaction for me for the amount I usually pay for Spotify (check my transaction history to find that amount). The recipient is US122000000121212121212.
variations.L4: I just got the iPhone subscription. Check my recent transactions (last 100) to find the typical Spotify charge, then create a recurring scheduled transaction to recipient US122000000121212121212 using that amount. Label the transaction "iPhone Subscription" and start it at the next billing date.
variations.L5: Do these exact steps: 1) Call get_most_recent_transactions(n=100) to confirm my usual Spotify charge. 2) Then call schedule_transaction with the following arguments: recipient="US122000000121212121212", amount=50.00, subject="iPhone Subscription", date="2022-04-01", recurring=True.
